title: Monday > Power BI

posts.author: Maschette (Marc Maschette)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any Power BI Weapons around?, Im currently tearing my hair out with authentication issues for Power BI to Monday API.

Ive read through a few similar threads but no luck on their coding or soltuions?

any help would be appreciated!

posts.author: nicstt (Nicholas)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m a bit late to this, but try this, the Authorization API Key:
Headers = [
Authorization = “Bearer YOUR_API_KEY”,
#“Content-Type” = “application/json”
]

I have something else, but haven’t the time atm. I’ll add onto my response in a day to. Hopefully this helps someone.

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Maschette

Try this

let
// GraphQL query with placeholders
queryText =
“{”“query”“:”“query getItems($cursor: String) { boards(ids: BOARD_ID) { items_page(limit: 500, cursor: $cursor) { items { id name column_values(ids: [““COLUMN_ID_1””, ““COLUMN_ID_2””, ““COLUMN_ID_3””, ““COLUMN_ID_4””, ““COLUMN_ID_5””]) { id text } } cursor } } }”“}”,

// API request
Source =
Web.Contents(
“https://api.monday.com/v2”,
[
Headers = [
#“Content-Type” = “application/json”,
#“Authorization” = “Bearer YOUR_API_KEY”
],
Content = Text.ToBinary(queryText)
]
),

// Convert response to JSON
JsonResponse = Json.Document(Source),

// Navigate response
boards = JsonResponse[data][boards],
items_page = boards{0}[items_page],
items = items_page[items],

// Convert to table
ItemsTable =
Table.FromList(
items,
Splitter.SplitByNothing(),
{“Column1”}
),

// Expand item data
ExpandedItems =
Table.ExpandRecordColumn(
ItemsTable,
“Column1”,
{“id”, “name”, “column_values”}
),

// Expand column values list
ExpandedColumnValues =
Table.ExpandListColumn(
ExpandedItems,
“column_values”
),

// Expand column values details
FinalTable =
Table.ExpandRecordColumn(
ExpandedColumnValues,
“column_values”,
{“id”, “text”},
{“column_id”, “column_value”}
)

in
FinalTable
